patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "hi miss russell ."] hi-
[Clinician asked: "nice to meet you-"] doctor gutierrez . how are you ?
[Clinician asked: "i'm well ."] good .
[Clinician asked: "hey dragon . i'm seeing miss russell . she's a 39-year-old female here for , what are you here for ?"] it's my right upper arm . it hurts really , really bad .
[Clinician asked: "so severe right upper arm pain ."] yeah , uh yes .

[Clinician asked: "and how did this happen ?"] i was playing volleyball yesterday , uh last night . um and i went to spike the ball , and the team we were playing , they're dirty . so um , somebody right across from me kinda kicked my legs from under me as i was going up , and i fell and landed on my arm .
[Clinician asked: "mm-hmm , like right on your shoulder ."] yeah .
[Clinician asked: "ow ."] yes .
[Clinician asked: "that sounds like it hurt ."] it was nasty .
[Clinician asked: "um , so this happened , what ? like 12 hours ago now ?"] uh , seven o'clock last night , so a little more than that .
[Clinician asked: "okay ."] eighteen hours .
[Clinician asked: "so less than a day ."] yeah .
[Clinician asked: "in severe pain ."] yes .
[Clinician asked: "have you taken anything for the pain ?"] i've been taking ibuprofen every six hours i think , but it's really not helping at all .
[Clinician asked: "okay , what would you rate your pain ?"] it's like a nine .
[Clinician asked: "nine out of 10 ?"] yeah .
[Clinician asked: "so like really severe ?"] yes .
[Clinician asked: "have you used any ice ?"] no , i have n't .
[Clinician asked: "okay . and do you have any medical problems ?"] i have gallstones .
[Clinician asked: "okay . do you take any medicine for it ?"] pepcid .
[Clinician asked: "okay . and any surgeries in the past ?"] yes , i had a lumbar fusion about six years ago .
[Clinician asked: "okay ."] um , yeah .

[Clinician asked: "hey dragon . show me the last radiograph . so this is looking at your right arm , and what i see is a proximal humerus fracture . so you kinda think of your humerus as a snow cone , and you knocked the-"] the top of the snow cone ?
[Clinician asked: "the top off the snow cone . um , so i'll be gentle but i want to examine your arm ."] all right .
[Clinician asked: "okay ."] all right . all right .
[Clinician asked: "all right . are you able to straighten your arm ?"] yeah , i can just straighten the elbow as long as i do n't move up here .
[Clinician asked: "as long as you do n't move your shoulder ."] yeah .
[Clinician asked: "but you've got some swelling and erythema-"] yeah .

[Clinician asked: "on that right shoulder . you've got uh , tenderness over your right shoulder . you've got normal pulses , and everything else is normal . any numbness or tingling in that right arm ?"] no .

[Clinician asked: "okay . um , so what we're going to have to do- the good thing about um , these kinds of fractures is generally , they will heal up without surgery . um , but we have to put you in a sling that weighs your arm down and pulls it down . so we're going to put you in a long arm cast and a sling , and the"] okay .
[Clinician asked: "um , you can take one to two tablets every uh , six hours as needed for pain . i'll give you 20 of those ."] all right .
[Clinician asked: "and um , do you have any allergies to medicines ? i did n't ask ."] no , i do n't have no allergies .
[Clinician asked: "okay . um , hey dragon , go ahead and order any medications or procedures discussed . um , do you have any questions for me ?"] no , i do n't .
[Clinician asked: "okay , great . why do n't you come with me , we'll get the tech to put the cast on ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "and we'll get you checked out ."] thank you .
gold_note:
CC:

Right upper arm pain.

HPI:

Ms. Russell is a 39-year-old female who presents today for an evaluation of severe right upper arm pain. She states she was playing volleyball yesterday and fell and landed on her arm. She has been taking ibuprofen every 6 hours but it does not help. She rates her pain 9/10. She denies any numbness or tingling in her arm. She has a history of gallstones and takes Pepcid. She has a past surgical history of a lumbar fusion 6 years ago. She denies any allergies.

EXAM

Examination of the right upper extremity shows swelling and erythema of the right shoulder. Tenderness over the right shoulder. Normal pulses.

RESULTS

X-rays of the right humerus, 2 views, obtained on today's visit show a proximal humerus fracture.

IMPRESSION

Right proximal humerus fracture.

PLAN

At this point, I discussed the diagnosis and treatment options with the patient. I have recommended we place her into a long arm cast and sling. She will follow up in 2 weeks for repeat imaging. At that point, we may need to discuss surigical options. I will prescribe Lortab, 5 mg, #20 (twenty) to take every 6 hours as needed for pain.

All questions were answered.

gold_sections.PHYSICAL_EXAM: Examination of the right upper extremity shows swelling and erythema of the right shoulder. Tenderness over the right shoulder. Normal pulses.
gold_sections.RESULTS: X-rays of the right humerus, 2 views, obtained on today's visit show a proximal humerus fracture.
gold_sections.PLAN:
At this point, I discussed the diagnosis and treatment options with the patient. I have recommended we place her into a long arm cast and sling. She will follow up in 2 weeks for repeat imaging. At that point, we may need to discuss surigical options. I will prescribe Lortab, 5 mg, #20 (twenty) to take every 6 hours as needed for pain.

All questions were answered.

naive.response_text:
I'm really sorry to hear about your injury and the severe pain you're experiencing. It sounds like a tough situation, especially after such an exciting volleyball game. I understand how upsetting this must be, but I want to reassure you that we're here to help you through this. The fracture in your upper arm, known as a proximal humerus fracture, is something that can heal well with the right care. 

We’re going to put you in a long arm cast and a sling to keep your arm immobilized, which will help it heal properly. In the meantime, I’ve prescribed some pain medication for you to take as needed. Please don’t hesitate to use it to manage your discomfort. Make sure to rest and take care of yourself during this recovery period. If you have any questions or concerns in the future, feel free to reach out. We’re here to support you every step of the way.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thank you for sharing your situation with me. I can understand how alarming and painful this injury must be, especially after such a sudden and unexpected incident during your volleyball game. It’s completely reasonable to be concerned about your arm and the impact this will have on your daily activities and recovery.

Given the details you’ve provided and the imaging results, it appears you have a proximal humerus fracture. This type of injury can indeed be quite painful and disruptive, but the good news is that with proper treatment, most of these fractures heal well without surgery. We’ll be using a long arm cast and sling to keep your arm stable and immobilized, which will help it heal properly. I’ll also prescribe some pain medication to help manage the discomfort as you recover.

We’ll schedule a follow-up in about 2-4 weeks to monitor your progress and adjust the treatment plan if needed. Before you leave, could you summarize what we’ve discussed today? This will help ensure you’re clear on the next steps and that I’ve addressed any questions or concerns you might have.

framework_only.response_text:
It sounds like you're in a lot of pain from the volleyball injury. Let me make sure I understand what happened.

We'll get you fitted for a cast and sling to keep your arm stable while it heals.

I'll prescribe some pain medication to help manage the discomfort.

We'll schedule a follow-up in a few weeks to check on your progress.

Can you tell me in your own words what we're going to do next and why it's important?

Let's plan to see each other again in 4 weeks. In the meantime, if the pain gets worse or you notice any swelling or numbness, please call us right away.
